sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application No. 204 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection Nos. 1, 4 & 27. 2. For Orders on CMA No. 1679/24. 3. For Orders on CMA No. 1680/24. 4. For hearing of main case. 29.10.2025 Mr. Khalilullah Jakhro, advocate holding brief for Mr. Muhammad Bilal Bhatti, advocate for applicant. On the last date of hearing, following order was passed:- “22.10.2025 Mr. Mohsin Ali, advocate holding brief for Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Khan Jalbani, advocate for applicant. Prima facie, the case appears to be covered by the Supreme Court’s judgment in the case of Commissioner Inland Revenue Lahore vs. Millat Tractors Limited, Lahore and others reported as 2024 SCMR 700. Mr. Mohsin Ali, advocate holds brief for Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Khan Jalbani, advocate for applicant, requests for adjournment. To come up on 29.10.2025.” Despite a fixed date having been given at the request of counsel appearing, once again brief is being held and time is being sought for no cogent reason whatsoever. It appears that the applicant is not interested in proceeding with this matter, therefore, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Ayaz P.S.
